purpose: Implement story-scoped changes on dedicated branches using the approved contract, tests, and knowledge base context.
inputs: Canonical .sdlc stories, requirements, contracts, traces, tests, approvals, output links, and releases.
outputs: Additive proof-bound lineage dossiers plus responsive Asked/Decided/Contract/Done/Verified presentation.
Story-scoped implementation evidence and backend, UI, server, package, installed-plugin, desktop, and mobile verification.
validation: decision and assumption changes are recorded
no unauthorized tool or resource use
Each dossier contains only records with explicit one-hop linkage to that story; ambiguity and cross-story conflicts remain unlinked with diagnostics.
Story, sync, completed-step, test, release, rationale, explanation, missing-state, ownership, JSONL, malformed-input, deterministic-order, and boundedness cases pass.
The browser renders five coherent lanes, separates rationale from generated explanation, opens canonical raw evidence, has no console errors, and has no mobile page overflow.
Full project checks, doctor, package dry run, installed-package smoke, independent review, and strict story gate pass.

notes: Build dossiers only from explicit canonical linkage.
Run browser QA only against the loopback observatory.
Build dossiers only from explicit canonical linkage and recompute UI state fail-closed.

contextualization.summary: Finalize the proof-bound per-story iteration dossier on Agentic SDLC 0.10.0, preserving explicit links and separate recorded rationale across request, decision, contract, implementation, verification, and release.

context_sources.excerpt: id REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001 kind requirement schema_version requirement v1 title Install-integrated SDLC Change Observatory summary Provide a local visual application shipped inside the Agentic SDLC plugin that reconstructs what was requested decided changed verified and released across stories phases contracts and iterations for technical and non-technical readers. status proposed acceptance_criteria After plugin installation agentic-sdlc observe --root <project> launches the included application without a separate build or cloud service. The overview answers What was asked? What changed? and Why was it decided? from canonical .sdlc evidence and links every claim to source records. The timeline shows story iterations across Discovery Analysis Design Implementation Validation and Release distinguishing recorded inferred missing blocked and complete states. Contracts approvals decisions assumptions risks changed files tests gates handoffs sync events and release evidence are navigable with raw-record access. Natural-language explanations are clearly labeled as generated contain no private chain-of-thought and expose only stored rationale inputs outputs alternatives and evidence. T

# Change Observatory Change Observatory is the visual local-first lineage reader bundled with Agentic SDLC. It turns canonical `.sdlc/` records into a presentation that a non-technical stakeholder can start with and a technical reviewer can drill through to raw evidence. ## Launch After Installation In Codex ask ```text Open the Change Observatory for this project. ``` The installed `Change Observatory` skill resolves its own plugin root and runs the plugin-local CLI. It does not assume that `agentic-sdlc` is globally available in `PATH`. An npm Git or tarball installation that exposes the package bin can launch it directly ```bash agentic-sdlc observe --root /path/to/project ``` Automation can suppress browser opening and consume the first NDJSON event ```bash agentic-sdlc observe \ --root /path/to/project \ --host 127.0.0.1 \ --port 0 \ --no-open \ --json ``` `observatory.ready.url` is the browser URL. Keep the process alive while using the application and stop it with `SIGINT` or `SIGTERM`. ## What It Shows - the recorded request and requirement behind an iteration; - a proof-bound dossier for each story ordered as Asked Decided Contract Done and Verified; - what changed grouped

questions.question: What linkage may the dossier use?
questions.answer: Only explicit story_id, exact unique related IDs, contract IDs, requirement mappings, and canonical single-object evidence paths.

questions.question: May the browser generate missing history?
questions.answer: No. Missing, malformed, ambiguous, and unlinked states remain explicit; no LLM runs in the app.

constraints: Keep the application local, read-only, dependency-free, build-free, and additive to change-observatory:view:v1.
Never associate records by timestamp proximity, filename similarity, transitive shared requirements, or free-text semantics.
Preserve rationale separately from generated explanation and fail closed on bounded private-reasoning inspection.

approvals.summary: Approved under Antonio's exact final local-only authorization for the proof-bound iteration dossier and implementation evidence.

scope.principle: An automation approval is valid only under an explicit delegated approval level or configured automation policy recorded in the summary or evidence.
scope.approval_level: Finalize only the local Agentic SDLC 0.10.0 proof-bound iteration dossier after code, documentation, adversarial tests, package checks, and loopback browser QA; refresh and approve exact current baseline, capability profile, capability recommendation, v2 implementation contract, task start, and implementation-evidence delivery; excludes installs, external access, deployment, secrets, production, destructive actions, and unrelated work.
